answer:
\boxed{B}  

The law likely implicates the dormant commerce clause by discriminating against out-of-state casino owners. However, it may be upheld if the state demonstrates that the law serves a legitimate local safety interest that outweighs any burden on interstate commerce. Since the statute targets all private casinos but disproportionately affects out-of-state owners, its constitutionality hinges on this balancing test.
